heading: Examination of and enforcement actions against designated financial market utilities
label: 5466 Examination of and enforcement actions against designated financial market utilities

children.text: The nature of the operations of, and the risks borne by, the designated financial market utility.
The financial and operational risks presented by the designated financial market utility to financial institutions, critical markets, or the broader financial system.
The resources and capabilities of the designated financial market utility to monitor and control such risks.
The safety and soundness of the designated financial market utility.

children.label: B
children.text: the rules and orders prescribed under this subchapter.

children.heading: Service providers
children.label: b Service providers
children.text: Whenever a service integral to the operation of a designated financial market utility is performed for the designated financial market utility by another entity, whether an affiliate or non-affiliate and whether on or off the premises of the designated financial market utility, the Supervisory Agency may examine whether the provision of that service is in compliance with applicable law, rules, orders, and standards to the same extent as if the designated financial market utility were performing the service on its own premises.

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: c Enforcement
children.text: For purposes of enforcing the provisions of this subchapter, a designated financial market utility shall be subject to, and the appropriate Supervisory Agency shall have authority under the provisions of subsections (b) through (n) of in the same manner and to the same extent as if the designated financial market utility was an insured depository institution and the Supervisory Agency was the appropriate Federal banking agency for such insured depository institution.section 1818 of this title

children.heading: Board of Governors involvement in examinations
children.label: d Board of Governors involvement in examinations

children.heading: Board of Governors consultation on examination planning
children.label: 1 Board of Governors consultation on examination planning
children.text: The Supervisory Agency shall consult annually with the Board of Governors regarding the scope and methodology of any examination conducted under subsections (a) and (b). The Supervisory Agency shall lead all examinations conducted under subsections (a) and (b)1So in original. Probably should be followed by a period.1

children.heading: Board of Governors participation in examination
children.label: 2 Board of Governors participation in examination
children.text: The Board of Governors may, in its discretion, participate in any examination led by a Supervisory Agency and conducted under subsections (a) and (b).

children.heading: Board of Governors enforcement recommendations
children.label: e Board of Governors enforcement recommendations

children.heading: Recommendation
children.label: 1 Recommendation
children.text: The Board of Governors may, after consulting with the Council and the Supervisory Agency, at any time recommend to the Supervisory Agency that such agency take enforcement action against a designated financial market utility in order to prevent or mitigate significant liquidity, credit, operational, or other risks to the financial markets or to the financial stability of the United States. Any such recommendation for enforcement action shall provide a detailed analysis supporting the recommendation of the Board of Governors.

children.heading: Consideration
children.label: 2 Consideration
children.text: The Supervisory Agency shall consider the recommendation of the Board of Governors and submit a response to the Board of Governors within 60 days.

children.heading: Binding arbitration
children.label: 3 Binding arbitration
children.text: If the Supervisory Agency rejects, in whole or in part, the recommendation of the Board of Governors, the Board of Governors may refer the recommendation to the Council for a binding decision on whether an enforcement action is warranted.

children.label: A
children.text: exercise the enforcement authority referenced in subsection (c); and

children.label: B
children.text: take enforcement action against the designated financial market utility.

children.heading: Emergency enforcement actions by the Board of Governors
children.label: f Emergency enforcement actions by the Board of Governors
i
children.text: an action engaged in, or contemplated by, a designated financial market utility (including any change proposed by the designated financial market utility to its rules, procedures, or operations that would otherwise be subject to ) poses an imminent risk of substantial harm to financial institutions, critical markets, or the broader financial system of the United States; orsection 5465(e) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the condition of a designated financial market utility poses an imminent risk of substantial harm to financial institutions, critical markets, or the broader financial system; and

children.label: B
children.text: the imminent risk of substantial harm precludes the Board of Governors’ use of the procedures in subsection (e).

children.heading: Enforcement authority
children.label: 2 Enforcement authority
children.text: For purposes of taking enforcement action under paragraph (1), a designated financial market utility shall be subject to, and the Board of Governors shall have authority under the provisions of subsections (b) through (n) of in the same manner and to the same extent as if the designated financial market utility was an insured depository institution and the Board of Governors was the appropriate Federal banking agency for such insured depository institution.2section 1818 of this titleSo in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.2
